objective: Créer un pipeline CI/CD complet pour MissionPulse avec build, release automatique vers Chrome Web Store et health checks des connecteurs

reason: Context files created, ready for parallel worker delegation

content: Strategy: Parallel delegation to @codegen (CI/CD) and @tests (health checks)
reason: Two independent workstreams - CI infrastructure and connector monitoring

content: Create comprehensive CI/CD pipeline for Chrome extension including: 1) Version bumping from git tags, 2) Build artifact with signed .zip, 3) Chrome Web Store publish on release, 4) Health check job to verify connectors work

Create connector health check system: 1) Script to test each connector by fetching real data, 2) CI job that runs periodically to detect broken connectors, 3) Reporting mechanism for failures, 4) Fixtures for expected responses

description: Workflow invoked Vitest directly instead of the dedicated health-check runner, so connector filtering/report parsing diverged from the script contract
resolution: Updated workflow to call `pnpm health-check:json`, parse `health-report.json`, and upload the report artifact

description: The runner imported `.health.test.ts` modules, which execute `test.describe()` at module load and crash outside Playwright
resolution: Extracted reusable connector runners into dedicated `*.health.ts` modules and pointed both tests and runner to those shared implementations

description: package.json CI/tooling dependencies were not reflected in pnpm-lock.yaml, causing `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` to fail
resolution: Regenerated pnpm-lock.yaml with pnpm and re-verified frozen-lockfile installation

content: Keep health-check reporting contract JSON-first in CI
reason: A machine-readable report is more stable for workflow parsing and issue/report generation than grepping human-readable test output

content: Expose connector health runners from non-test modules under `tests/health/connectors/*.health.ts`
reason: Separates reusable runtime logic from Playwright's test registration side effects while preserving existing test files

description: Fixed badge typing/syntax and removed dead runner imports
reason: Restored executable TypeScript and simplified the health-reporting path without changing behavior

content: Declare explicit read-only permissions in ci.yml
reason: Reduces default GitHub token scope and aligns CI with least-privilege workflow practices
Security hardening without changing CI behavior

description: No timeout-minutes on any of the 6 CI jobs (setup, lint, format, typecheck, test, build, test-e2e). A stuck job runs for 6 hours by default. release.yml and connector-health.yml define timeouts correctly.
publish-to-chrome-store job has no timeout-minutes. CWS publish could hang indefinitely.
Changelog generation uses deprecated %25/%0A/%0D escaping for multiline GITHUB_OUTPUT. Since 2022-10, GitHub Actions requires EOF-delimiter syntax. Changelog with newlines will be silently truncated.
Dead import: runFreeWorkHealthCheck imported but never used. Same pattern in all 5 *.health.test.ts files.
Supabase anon key hardcoded in 3 places (hiway.health.ts, hiway.health.test.ts, hiway.connector.ts). Not a secret (anon role), but should be centralized to avoid drift.
Actions pinned to major tags (@v4, @v2) not SHA hashes. Acceptable but not best practice for supply-chain security.
Full Changelog link in release body is incorrect: compare/${{ github.ref_name }}...HEAD should be compare/PREVIOUS_TAG...${{ github.ref_name }}

content: Re-verified 3 major violations from run 4: all fixed. ci.yml has timeout-minutes on all 7 jobs (5/10/5/10/15/15/20). release.yml publish-to-chrome-store has timeout-minutes: 10. Changelog uses EOF delimiter syntax.
reason: Post-fix validation confirms corrections are properly applied

content: All 3 major violations fixed: timeouts added to ci.yml and release.yml, EOF delimiter syntax used for changelog
